Home/Thin Stone Veneer

Thin Stone Veneer Installation in Austin, TX

Add the beauty and permanence of natural stone to your Austin home — without the structural weight or cost of full masonry. Star Stucco installs thin stone veneer on fireplaces, accent walls, facades, and outdoor living spaces across Central Texas.

Get a Free Estimate (512) 706-9699

Natural Stone Character for Austin Homes

The Hill Country has been defined by stone for centuries — from the limestone bluffs along the Colorado River to the hand-laid rock walls of early Texas homesteads. Thin stone veneer brings that tradition to modern construction, giving homeowners in Westlake Hills, Barton Creek, Bee Cave, and across the Austin metro a practical way to add real stone to their homes.

Unlike full-thickness stone masonry, thin veneer is cut or cast to approximately one inch thick, reducing weight by up to 75 percent. That means it can be installed on standard wood-framed and steel-stud walls without special footings or structural reinforcement — opening up applications that full masonry cannot reach, from upper stories and interior walls to lightweight outdoor kitchen islands.

Star Stucco carries both natural thin-cut stone — quarried limestone, sandstone, and granite in profiles that echo the local geology — and high-quality manufactured stone that replicates the look and texture of natural stone at a lower cost. We help you choose the right material for your project, then install it with the same attention to craft and durability we bring to every stucco project.

Why Stone Veneer Works in Austin

Austin homeowners benefit from stone veneer in several ways. Stone is inherently fireproof — a real asset in a region prone to wildfire risk. It shrugs off UV exposure that degrades painted surfaces, and it is completely impervious to the insects and moisture that threaten wood siding. With proper installation including a drainage plane and flexible mortar joints, thin stone veneer handles Austin\'s thermal cycling and clay-soil movement without cracking.

Stone also adds measurable resale value. In Austin\'s competitive real estate market, a stone fireplace surround, a stone-accented entry, or a full stone facade distinguishes a home from comparable listings and commands a premium.

Our Installation Process

  1. 1Design consultation and stone selection
  2. 2Surface preparation and moisture barrier
  3. 3Scratch coat application (if needed)
  4. 4Stone layout and hand placement
  5. 5Grouting and joint finishing
  6. 6Sealing, cleanup, and final walkthrough

Ready to start?

Request a Free Quote

Stone Veneer Applications

Thin stone veneer works in more places than most homeowners realize. Here are the applications we install most often across the Austin area.

Fireplace Surrounds

A stone fireplace transforms any living room. We install stacked stone, ledgestone, and limestone surrounds that become the centerpiece of the space — from modern linear designs in East Austin lofts to floor-to-ceiling stonework in Westlake Hills homes.

Exterior Accent Walls & Facades

Stone veneer on an entry wall, chimney chase, or full facade elevates curb appeal and adds lasting value. We select stone profiles that complement your home's architecture, whether Hill Country ranch, Spanish Colonial, or contemporary.

Outdoor Kitchens & Living Areas

Austin's outdoor lifestyle makes stone-clad outdoor kitchens, bar surrounds, and seating walls a popular investment. Thin stone veneer stands up to the elements while giving outdoor spaces a permanent, finished look.

Interior Accent Walls

A stone accent wall in a bedroom, dining room, or home office adds texture and warmth. Thin veneer keeps the weight manageable on interior walls and pairs beautifully with the warm wood and neutral palettes popular in Austin interiors.

Columns, Pillars & Entryways

Stone-wrapped columns and entry pillars create a distinguished first impression. We wrap existing wood or steel columns with stone veneer that looks like solid masonry, adding character to porches, porticos, and driveways.

Retaining Walls & Landscape Features

Thin stone veneer applied to retaining walls, planters, and seat walls ties hardscape into the natural Hill Country landscape. We use stone colors and textures that complement the native limestone and granite of the Austin area.

Why Choose Star Stucco for Stone Veneer

Hill Country Stone Expertise

We know Austin's architectural landscape and select stone profiles that complement Hill Country, Spanish Colonial, and modern styles.

Natural & Manufactured Stone

We carry both natural thin-cut stone and high-quality manufactured profiles, giving you options for every budget and aesthetic.

Custom Color & Layout

Every installation is custom. We arrange stone by hand, selecting each piece for color, texture, and fit to create a natural, non-repetitive look.

Fire & Weather Resistant

Stone veneer is fireproof, UV-resistant, and impervious to insects and rot — ideal for Austin's hot summers and occasional severe weather.

Natural Stone vs. Manufactured Stone

Both options deliver excellent results, and we install both daily. The choice comes down to aesthetics, budget, and application.

Natural Thin-Cut Stone

Quarried stone sliced to veneer thickness. Every piece is unique, with authentic color variation and texture that manufactured stone works hard to replicate. Natural limestone and sandstone from Texas quarries pair beautifully with Hill Country architecture. Slightly heavier and more expensive than manufactured, but unmatched in authenticity.

Manufactured Stone Veneer

Cast from molds of real stone using Portland cement, aggregates, and iron oxide pigments. Modern manufactured stone is remarkably realistic — most people cannot tell the difference once it is installed and grouted. It is lighter, more uniform in thickness (which speeds installation), and typically 20-40% less expensive than natural stone. Available in a wide range of profiles from stacked ledgestone to tumbled fieldstone.
